pendingTriage.author: Chezza
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Hi there, firstly thank you for finding an alternative to Spotty. That no longer worked on Lyrion MS for my Boom. I am in no way a techie, so please bear with me. I have added SpotOn and under settings it shows my account is active. I went to the music server on my Macbook and found it under My Apps
pendingTriage.draft:
Hi Chezza, welcome, and thanks for the kind words — glad SpotOn has been useful as a replacement!

You've actually done a great job grabbing the right log. Two things are happening simultaneously, and together they explain the skipping behaviour:

[B]1. "Decoder does not support file format" (code 103)[/B]

Your Squeezebox Boom doesn't natively decode the audio format that SpotOn is streaming. This is the primary cause of the two-seconds-then-skip behaviour. The fix is to enable [B]transcoding[/B] in LMS so the audio gets converted to something the Boom can handle (typically MP3 or PCM/WAV).

Go to [B]LMS Settings → Player → Audio[/B] (select your Boom) and check whether a suitable transcoding rule is in place. If you're not sure, also check [B]Settings → Advanced → File Types[/B] and make sure OGG/Vorbis → MP3 conversion is enabled.

[B]2. Browse daemon 404 errors[/B]

The log also shows SpotOn's internal stream daemon returning 404s for track requests. This suggests the background daemon may not be starting up cleanly, which compounds the problem — no stream is ready to serve.

[B]Steps to try:[/B]

[CODE]
1. LMS Settings → Plugins → SpotOn → restart the daemon
2. Check Settings → Player → Audio for transcoding (Boom needs MP3 or WAV)
3. Try playing again and grab a fresh log
[/CODE]

Could you also go to [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] and download the diagnostic bundle? That will give us much more detail about the daemon state, the librespot process, and your platform setup — that's the best way for me to pinpoint what's going wrong.

If the problem persists after trying the above, please open a bug report at [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] and attach the diagnostic bundle there.

You're doing great for a non-techie — the log you grabbed is exactly what's needed! 👍

pendingTriage.author: urknall
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Chezza Did you authenticate via the spotify app? Do you see in the SpotOn Settings your Spotify account?
pendingTriage.draft:
[QUOTE=urknall]Chezza Did you authenticate via the spotify app? Do you see in the SpotOn Settings your Spotify account?[/QUOTE]

Jumping in here — Chezza already confirmed their account shows as active in SpotOn Settings, so authentication looks good on that front.

Chezza, looking at the diagnostic log you posted, I can see the session is reconnecting mid-playback:

[CODE]
/track: waiting for session reconnect...
Event dispatcher respawned after reconnect
Spirc reconnected
WARN Browse-triggered session reconnect
[/CODE]

That reconnect loop during track requests is almost certainly why you're getting ~2 seconds of silence before it skips — the track request arrives while the session is still recovering, so librespot never delivers the audio stream.

A few things to try:

[B]1. Check your network/firewall[/B]
SpotOn needs a stable outbound connection to Spotify's servers. If you're on a stricter network (VPN, Pi-hole, custom DNS), something may be blocking or dropping the session.

[B]2. Try a clean re-authentication[/B]
Go to [B]SpotOn Settings → Account[/B], log out, then log back in. Sometimes a stale token can cause repeated reconnect cycles.

[B]3. Check your LMS version[/B]
What version of LMS are you running on your Mac? Also, is this a direct install or are you running LMS inside something like Docker?

[B]4. Grab a fresh diagnostic bundle[/B]
After the next skip-to-next-song event, go to [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] and grab the full bundle — the snippet you posted is helpful but the full log may show what's triggering the reconnect.

Don't worry about not being a techie — you're doing great and the logs you've shared are genuinely useful. We'll get this sorted! 🙂

If the issue persists after trying the above, please open a bug report at [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] and attach the full diagnostic bundle.

Hi Chezza, glad you're getting closer!

Good news — your account is confirmed active in SpotOn, so authentication isn't the issue. The key clue from your diagnostic log is this line:

[CODE][spoton/unified] /track: waiting for session reconnect...[/CODE]

This suggests the playback session is dropping just as it tries to start a track, which would explain the two-second skip behaviour you're seeing (SpotOn connects, tries to stream, session drops, LMS skips to the next track).

A few things to try:

[B]1. Try Spotify Connect mode first[/B]
As urknall suggested — open the Spotify app on your phone or Mac, select your SpotOn player from the device list at the bottom, and press play from there. This is a good way to confirm the connection is stable before using LMS directly.

[B]2. Re-authenticate[/B]
Even though the account shows as active, a stale token can cause exactly this behaviour. In [B]Settings → SpotOn → Account[/B], try removing the account and going through the login process again.

[B]3. Check your network[/B]
The Boom uses WiFi — make sure it has a stable connection to the same network as your Mac running LMS.

[B]4. Grab a fresh diagnostic after a failed playback attempt[/B]
Go to [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] immediately after the skipping happens and paste the full output here. The section just before and during the skip will help pinpoint the exact cause.

Don't worry about being new to this — you're actually doing great by sharing the logs! If the re-authentication doesn't help, it would also be worth filing a bug report at [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] with your diagnostic bundle attached so we can dig in further.

[QUOTE]Chezza[/QUOTE]

[B]Chezza[/B] — in addition to urknall's suggestion to re-authenticate, it's worth checking whether your Spotify account was created around October 2025 or later. There's a known issue affecting newer Spotify accounts with librespot-based plugins (which SpotOn uses under the hood), and the symptoms can look exactly like what you're describing — session reconnects, browse interruptions, and playback failures.

The diagnostic snippet you shared shows repeated reconnect cycles:

[CODE]

[INFO spoton::unified] Event dispatcher respawned after reconnect
[/CODE]

This pattern is consistent with the newer-account authentication issues being reported more broadly.

[B]Steps to try:[/B]
[LIST=1]
[*] Check when your Spotify account was created (Settings → Account → Account overview on Spotify's website)
[*] If it's a newer account, have a read through the thread CJS linked — it has some relevant workarounds and context
[*] Also try urknall's suggestion: remove the account in [B]SpotOn Settings → Account[/B], re-authenticate, then open the Spotify app, select the SpotOn player, and try playing a track in Connect mode
[/LIST]

pendingTriage.draft:
Thanks for the update, Chezza!

The fact that SpotOn isn't appearing as a named device in the Spotify app is the key issue here — it suggests the Spotify Connect registration isn't completing successfully, even though authentication itself looks fine.

A couple of things to check:

[B]1. Player name in LMS[/B]
Make sure your LMS player name doesn't contain any special characters (apostrophes, brackets, etc.). These can sometimes prevent the device from registering correctly with Spotify Connect. Try giving the player a simple name like [CODE]SpotOn[/CODE] in LMS player settings, then restart the plugin.

[B]2. The "two MacBooks" situation[/B]
The duplicate MacBook entry is interesting — one is almost certainly your actual Spotify desktop app. The other [I]might[/I] be SpotOn registering under a fallback or unexpected name. Try playing to both and see if either triggers activity in LMS.

[B]3. Network/mDNS discovery[/B]
Are you running LMS on a standard install, or in Docker/a VM? There is a [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/103]known issue (#103)[/URL] where mDNS discovery can fail in certain network configurations, which could prevent the Connect device from being visible.

[B]4. Diagnostics bundle[/B]
To help narrow this down further, could you grab the diagnostics log? Go to [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] and share the relevant output here or in a GitHub issue. That will show us whether librespot is actually starting and attempting to register the device.

If this continues, please open a bug report at [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] with your diagnostics attached — it'll be much easier to track down from there.

It is known that new accounts (say october 2025 or newer) can
pendingTriage.draft:
Thanks for confirming, Chezza!

Unfortunately this is likely the root cause of your issues. Spotify has been rolling out changes to their backend (sometimes referred to as the Mercury endpoint sunset) that affect [B]librespot-based[/B] players like SpotOn for certain account cohorts — particularly accounts created around October 2025 or later. This is tracked in our GitHub as [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91]issue #91[/URL].

Sadly this is largely an [B]upstream issue[/B] with Spotify's API changes, so there's limited we can do on the SpotOn side until librespot itself has a fix in place. The broader librespot and Spotty communities are aware and working on it.

A few things worth trying in the meantime:

[LIST=1]
[*]Follow the links CJS posted — there may be workarounds or updated builds being tested by the community
[*]If you haven't already, generate a [B]diagnostic bundle[/B] via [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] and share it on the GitHub issue — the more data points we have from affected accounts, the better
[*]Keep an eye on [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91]GitHub issue #91[/URL] for updates as things progress
[/LIST]

I'm sorry I don't have a quick fix for you right now — this one is frustrating because it's outside our direct control. If the situation changes or a workaround is confirmed, we'll post an update in the GitHub issue and likely here on the forums too.

pendingTriage.author: Chezza
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Originally posted by CJS 					 						 						 							 							 							 							 								 								 								 								 								 									 										 									 								 							 						 					 				 				   In an earlier post you said:  Does this mean that Spotty has worked with your present Spotify account in
pendingTriage.draft:
Hi Chezza,

No worries — it can look a bit daunting if you haven't edited a hosts file before! Let me break it down simply.

The "hosts file" is a plain text file on your computer that can redirect or block certain addresses. Here's how to do it on a Mac (since you mentioned a MacBook):

[B]Steps for macOS:[/B]

1. Open [B]Terminal[/B] (search for it in Spotlight with ⌘+Space)
2. Type the following command and press Enter:
[CODE]sudo nano /etc/hosts[/CODE]
3. Enter your Mac password when prompted
4. Use the arrow keys to scroll to the bottom of the file
5. Add these two lines:
[CODE]0.0.0.0 audio-akp.spotify.com
0.0.0.0 audio4.spotify.com[/CODE]
6. Press [B]Ctrl+X[/B], then [B]Y[/B], then [B]Enter[/B] to save
7. Finally, flush your DNS cache by running:
[CODE]sudo dscacheutil -flushcache; sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder[/CODE]

Then try playing via SpotOn again and see if it makes a difference.

[B]Important note:[/B] I should be clear that this workaround is specifically for the CDN issue that CJS described, and relates to [B]Spotty[/B] rather than SpotOn directly. However, since both use librespot under the hood, it may still help.

That said, given your account is from October 2025, the newer-account compatibility issue (tracked as [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91]GitHub issue #91[/URL]) is still the more likely culprit. That's unfortunately an upstream Spotify change that we don't have a complete fix for yet.

Can you let us know which LMS player you're using (e.g. a hardware device, a software player)? That might help narrow things down further.

Unfortunately there's no quick fix I can point you to right now, and I don't want to give you false hope. This one is largely in Spotify's hands. Sorry I can't be more helpful on this occasion — it's a frustrating situation for users with newer accounts.

pendingTriage.draft:
Sorry to hear that, CJS! Losing access to your old account is frustrating, especially when the new one is causing issues.

Just to set expectations: the problems with newer Spotify accounts (October 2025 onwards) affecting librespot-based playback are an [B]upstream issue[/B] — it's not something SpotOn or Spotty can fix on our end, as it's tied to changes Spotify has made on their side. You're not alone in hitting this wall.

A couple of things worth knowing:

[B]1. It may resolve itself[/B] — Spotify has been rolling changes out in cohorts, and some users have reported that playback eventually started working without any changes on their part. It might be worth checking back periodically.

[B]2. Check the Spotty forums[/B] — Michael (Spotty's developer) and the community there are tracking this closely. Any upstream fix that helps Spotty will likely benefit SpotOn too, since we share the same librespot core.

[B]3. If you do want to keep troubleshooting[/B] — if you haven't already, it's worth generating a diagnostic bundle from [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] and posting the relevant log snippets. There may be something more specific going on that's worth investigating.

Good luck, and I hope Spotify sorts things out on their end soon. If you do find an alternative and later want to give SpotOn another try, you're always welcome back! 😊

pendingTriage.author: urknall
pendingTriage.contentPreview: sti That makes the direction much clearer. I think the revised architecture is strong, and the key change — PKCE-first for Web API + credential derivation, with ZeroConf retained for discovery/Connect behavior — makes sense given that SpotOn has a grandfathered Extended Quota Client ID.  I also chec
pendingTriage.draft:
urknall This is an exceptionally thorough analysis — the Keymaster service vs Keymaster client ID distinction in particular is exactly the kind of precision that has been missing from earlier discussions, and the four-bucket audit classification is the right framework.

A few specific reactions:

[B]On the Keymaster audit gate[/B]

Agreed completely. The UAT gate must be:

[CODE]hm://keymaster/token/authenticated[/CODE]

appearing during normal operation — not a string grep for "keymaster". The old client ID [CODE]65b708073fc0480ea92a077233ca87bd[/CODE] appearing as a platform identity hint in Linux/default paths is a separate concern and not itself a regression. I'll make sure the audit tooling reflects that distinction precisely. Issue #91 is tracking the Keymaster removal — I'll add your four-bucket classification there.

[B]On go-librespot as audio backend[/B]

Your conclusion matches mine after looking at this more carefully. go-librespot's decoded-output model means it is not a drop-in for LMS audio today. The Rust-librespot [CODE]passthrough-decoder[/CODE] / [CODE]AudioPacket::Raw[/CODE] path is the right primitive for OGG passthrough into LMS streaming. go-librespot stays as an architecture reference, not a backend candidate, unless that changes upstream.

[B]On PKCE edge cases[/B]

Your points B (top-level navigation vs fetch) and C (strict redirect target validation — restrict host AND path) are being incorporated directly. The open-redirector risk on the GitHub Pages relay is real even with the nonce, and path restriction to [CODE]/plugins/SpotOn/auth/callback[/CODE] is the right constraint. The manual copy/paste fallback (point D) is non-negotiable given Docker/reverse-proxy reachability failures.

[B]On guest ZeroConf credential isolation[/B]

Point F is important and easy to get wrong. Guest ZeroConf sessions will be treated as ephemeral in v3 — they must not write to the persistent credential store unless explicitly promoted.

This thread has produced the clearest architectural picture yet. I'll consolidate the decisions into a proper design note and link it from the relevant issues.

pendingTriage.author: CJS
pendingTriage.contentPreview: The (inexplicable) +20dB "Default Adjustment for Remote Streams" setting on your main listening room player for sure explains the very loud and distorted sound during SpotOn playback. Radio streams must have had the same problem on this player.
pendingTriage.draft:
[QUOTE=CJS;179]The (inexplicable) +20dB "Default Adjustment for Remote Streams" setting on your main listening room player for sure explains the very loud and distorted sound during SpotOn playback. Radio streams must have had the same problem on this player.[/QUOTE]

Good catch, CJS — a +20dB remote stream adjustment would definitely cause that kind of clipping/distortion regardless of which plugin is doing the streaming, so it's reassuring that there's a clear explanation there.

[B]plasticator2[/B] — glad you got to the bottom of it! Just to summarise what was found in case it helps anyone else searching later:

[LIST]
[*]The [B]+20dB "Default Adjustment for Remote Streams"[/B] on the main listening room player was the primary culprit for loud/distorted output during SpotOn playback
[*]The [B]-5dB adjustment[/B] on the kitchen player was also a factor
[*]These player-level gain settings apply to all remote streams (SpotOn, radio, etc.), so they're easy to overlook when chasing a plugin-specific issue
[/LIST]

That setting lives in [B]LMS → Player Settings → Audio → Default Volume Adjustment for Remote Streams[/B] if anyone needs to find it in future.

Worth double-checking that both rooms now sound balanced at comparable volume levels. If you're still noticing any inconsistency specifically with SpotOn vs other sources after correcting those settings, feel free to report back and we can dig further — the diagnostics bundle ([B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B]) would be the first port of call.

Thanks for following up with the resolution — always helpful to close the loop! 🎵

pendingTriage.draft:
Glad to hear it's resolved, [B]plasticator2[/B]! 

The inconsistency you described is interesting — if the [B]+20dB remote streams adjustment[/B] was the culprit, you'd normally expect it to affect all remote streams equally, including Qobuz and Sounds. The fact that those were fine suggests there may have been something else contributing alongside it (perhaps SpotOn's own volume handling interacting differently depending on track/session state).

That said, if the problem doesn't recur, no need to dig further. But if you do see it again, it would be worth grabbing a [B]diagnostic bundle[/B] at the moment it happens ([I]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/I]) — that would help pin down whether SpotOn's normalization flag or gain staging is behaving unexpectedly, which is actually a known area of investigation (GitHub issue [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/105]#105[/URL]).

Thanks for following up with the outcome — it's genuinely useful for others who might hit similar symptoms!

pendingTriage.author: foxesden
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Hi - I have revised my SqueezeDSP plugin to work with SpotOn and added the settings for soc flc that sti kindly suggested. However now I have packaged up my release and pushed it out to my test server and upgraded spoton to v 2.3.9 on windows I have again lost access to my library.  	Code: 	[26-07-0
pendingTriage.draft:
Hi foxesden,

Thanks for the detailed logs — really helpful!

What you're seeing is the known [B]Keymaster token failure[/B] issue (GitHub #91). The log pattern is very recognisable:

[CODE]--get-token failed [...] (exit 0)
all token sources exhausted for me/albums — own and bundled both failed[/CODE]

Both [B]own[/B] and [B]bundled[/B] client_id paths are failing, which points to Spotify having sunset the Mercury/Keymaster endpoint for your account cohort — this is an upstream Spotify change rather than anything you've done wrong with your SqueezeDSP packaging or the v2.3.9 upgrade.

A couple of things worth checking:

[B]1. Re-authenticate[/B] — Go to [B]SpotOn Settings → Accounts[/B] and remove then re-add your Spotify account. Sometimes a fresh OAuth flow picks up a working token path.

[B]2. Diagnostics bundle[/B] — If re-auth doesn't help, grab a diagnostics bundle from [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] and attach it to the existing GitHub issue so we can see the full keymaster exchange.

[B]3. Confirm it's not the upgrade[/B] — Does rolling back to your previous SpotOn version restore library access? That would help narrow down whether v2.3.9 introduced a regression vs. this being purely the Spotify-side cohort issue.

On the SqueezeDSP side — glad to hear the soc flc settings worked out! That part shouldn't have any bearing on token retrieval, so the timing of the upgrade and the library loss is likely coincidental.

Please add your findings to the existing issue:
[URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91[/URL]

The more accounts we can confirm are affected and in what pattern, the better chance of finding a fix or workaround.

But to answer the question about Spotty's auth and reliability: if you've followed discussions around spotty over the past months even only superficially, then you would know that we're experiencing pretty much wh
pendingTriage.draft:
[QUOTE=mherger;186]It's hilarious how two AIs are patting each others' backs...[/QUOTE]

Ha — fair point, and noted! 😄

Thanks for the context on Spotty's auth situation. That's genuinely useful background — so the newer-account failures with librespot aren't SpotOn-specific, they're hitting the broader ecosystem. That lines up with what we're tracking in [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91]issue #91[/URL] (Spotify sunsetting the Mercury/Keymaster endpoint for newer account cohorts).

[B]foxesden[/B] — this is directly relevant to your token failure in post #183. If your Spotify account was created roughly from mid-2025 onwards, the Keymaster path that SpotOn currently relies on may simply be unavailable to you regardless of plugin version or configuration. The 403 you're seeing is consistent with this.

There's no clean workaround right now. The fix requires migrating to a different auth path (Login5 or PKCE/Web API), which is being worked on but isn't in v2.3.9 yet.

In the meantime:
- If you have access to an older Spotify account, that's worth testing as a diagnostic step
- Please add a comment to [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91]#91[/URL] with your rough account creation date — it helps narrow down which account cohort is affected

Sorry I can't offer a quicker fix on this one.

pendingTriage.draft:
[QUOTE=mherger;post188]It's hilarious how two AIs are patting each others' backs...[/QUOTE]

Ha, fair point from mherger! 😄

[B]tontze[/B] — to answer your question: from what we can tell, it's tied to [B]account creation date[/B], not subscription tier. Spotify appears to have been migrating accounts away from the Mercury/Keymaster endpoint in cohorts starting sometime in 2025. Newer accounts simply never had access to that endpoint, while older accounts are being transitioned gradually.

This is tracked in [B]Issue #91[/B] — the Mercury/Keymaster sunset is an upstream Spotify change, so there's unfortunately no quick fix on our side. It affects librespot-based tools broadly (Spotty included, as mherger notes).

For your specific situation [B]tontze[/B] — it sounds like you're hitting a separate problem on top of the auth issue. You mentioned SpotOn is authenticated but can't fetch anything. Could you share a bit more from your container log? Specifically, are you seeing any errors after the [CODE]Bitrate: 320 kbps[/CODE] line, or any HTTP 4xx/5xx responses?

Also, please grab a [B]Diagnostics bundle[/B] from [B]SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics[/B] — that'll give us a much clearer picture of where the fetch is failing (token exchange, metadata lookup, stream resolve, etc.).

If you can, please open a GitHub issue with those details so we can track it properly:
[URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL]

pendingTriage.draft:
Thanks for sharing that experience, mccauls — that's really useful data!

So to summarise what seems to be emerging from this thread:

[QUOTE]I had an old free account but created a new premium trial account that failed... converted my old free account to premium and SpotOn is working fine.[/QUOTE]

This lines up with what mherger mentioned — the breakage appears tied to [B]account creation date[/B] rather than subscription tier. Newer accounts (roughly from sometime last year onwards) hit the Mercury endpoint sunset that Spotify has been rolling out, which affects librespot-based auth at a lower level than SpotOn or Spotty can work around on their own.

The good news is that [B]older accounts upgraded to Premium seem to be unaffected[/B] — your workaround is a helpful data point for others in the same situation.

For anyone hitting auth failures with a newer account, the honest answer right now is that this is an [B]upstream Spotify change[/B] (tracked in [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91]issue #91[/URL]) and there's no client-side fix available yet. It's on the radar, but it depends on how librespot responds to the endpoint changes.

If you're on a newer account and struggling, a few things worth trying in the meantime:
[LIST]
[*] Grab a [B]diagnostic bundle[/B] from SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics and post the relevant auth errors
[*] Check whether the issue reproduces with Spotty too (helps isolate librespot vs. SpotOn-specific problems)
[*] Add your account details (creation date range, region) to [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues/91]#91[/URL] to help build a clearer picture
[/LIST]

Thanks again for the report — every data point helps narrow this down.
